completion_criteria_checked: scripts/advance-cockpit-state.ps1 accepts an explicit archived-directive-path input and, when supplied, sets last_verified_handoff to that archived path rather than the live current_directive_path; when not supplied, existing behavior is preserved.
docs/VERIFICATION_RESULT_SPEC.md's next_action field definition is updated for PASS-verdict durable-statement semantics.
The documented close-out order is updated to archive before advancing, passing the archive path in.
A demonstration shows a full cycle leaves last_verified_handoff and next_action correct with no manual correction needed afterward.
The change stays within the approved V1 scope, remains local deterministic, and does not introduce broad orchestration or automation.
Claude returns evidence in .cockpit/result/worker-result.md using the required format.
evidence_reviewed: git diff --stat across all four changed files

independent scratch-copy reproduction with a different synthetic task_id (pcc-v1-verify-777) and a deliberately-planted stale-checklist next_action string, confirming it does not leak into task-state.json for a PASS verdict
independent scratch-copy test of the BLOCKED verdict path (not tested by the worker), confirming non-PASS next_action/current_blocker behavior is unaffected by the fix
git diff -- docs/DECISIONS.md (confirmed empty - out-of-scope file correctly left untouched)
risks: The fallback to old live-path behavior when -ArchivedDirectivePath is omitted means a verifier who forgets to archive-then-advance in the new order gets silently the old, still-slightly-wrong behavior rather than a warning.
The new default PASS next_action is a fixed generic template; richer per-cycle context requires remembering to pass -FinalNextAction explicitly.
The new close-out order is prose guidance only, not enforced by any script.

Self-verification note (DECISION-019): mitigated by independently reproducing the fix from a fresh scratch copy with different synthetic data (a different task_id, a different deliberately-planted stale string) than the worker used, and by independently testing a verdict path (BLOCKED) the worker's own testing did not cover.
summary: Independently reproduced the core fix from a fresh scratch copy using a different synthetic task_id and a different deliberately-planted stale-checklist next_action string, confirming it does not leak through for a PASS verdict and that the archived path is used correctly. Also independently tested the BLOCKED verdict path (not covered by the worker's own tests) and confirmed non-PASS behavior is unaffected. Confirmed via diff that docs/DECISIONS.md was correctly left untouched, matching allowed scope. All completion criteria met; no out-of-scope changes found.
next_action: Task 'pcc-v1-014' is complete and verified PASS. Owner/advisor selects and drafts the next bounded task.
